problem text: Suppose $X$ and $Y$ are random variables with standard deviations $1$ and $3$, respectively. The standard deviation of the sum $X+Y$ is $4$. Determine $\text{Corr}(X,Y)$.
problem solution: Using the formula for the variance of a sum, we have $\text{Var}(X+Y) = \text{Var}(X) + \text{Var}(Y) + 2\text{Cov}(X,Y)$. Substituting the given values, $4^2 = 1^2 + 3^2 + 2\rho (3)(1)$, which simplifies to $6\rho = 6$, thus $\rho = 1$.
